title: Chana Masala
cScaleSummary: C0 because the recipe contains no cholesterol-contributing animal-derived ingredients. The chickpeas, onion, tomato, garlic, ginger, spices, oil, lemon juice, cilantro, and rice or flatbread served alongside contribute 0 mg cholesterol as written.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active because the dish is built around chickpeas, a legume base rich in soluble fiber, with tomato, onion, aromatics, spices, herbs, acid, and a restrained amount of unsaturated oil providing flavor and structure without cholesterol-containing ingredients.
intro: Chana masala is one of the great North Indian chickpea dishes: hearty, tomato-rich, sharp with ginger and garlic, and built around a spice base that makes pantry ingredients feel like a real meal. It is often served with rice, roti, or bhatura, but the heart of the dish is the contrast between tender chickpeas and a deeply cooked onion-tomato masala. The best version should be bold, tangy, earthy, and spoonable, with enough sauce to coat the chickpeas without turning soupy.

stepGroups.title: Soak the Chickpeas
items: Place the chickpeas ({{quantity:dried-chickpeas}}) in a large bowl and cover with several inches of water.
Soak overnight.
Drain and rinse the chickpeas.

stepGroups.title: Cook the Chickpeas
items: Add the soaked chickpeas, water ({{quantity:water-cooking}}), and bay leaf ({{quantity:bay-leaf}}) to a pot.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to a steady simmer.
Cook for 60 to 90 minutes, until the chickpeas are tender but not falling apart.
Reserve the chickpea cooking liquid ({{quantity:chickpea-cooking-liquid}}), then drain the chickpeas.
Discard the bay leaf.

stepGroups.title: Brown the Onion Base
items: Heat the olive oil ({{quantity:olive-oil}}) in a large skillet or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the onion ({{quantity:yellow-onion}}) and fine sea salt ({{quantity:fine-sea-salt-onion}}).
Cook for 12 to 15 minutes, stirring often, until the onion is soft, golden, and beginning to brown at the edges.
Add the garlic ({{quantity:garlic}}), ginger ({{quantity:fresh-ginger}}), and serrano chile ({{quantity:serrano-chile}}).
Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.

stepGroups.title: Build the Masala
items: Add the tomato paste ({{quantity:tomato-paste}}).
Cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the tomato paste darkens and starts to stick to the pan.

Add the cumin ({{quantity:ground-cumin}}), coriander ({{quantity:ground-coriander}}), Kashmiri chile powder ({{quantity:kashmiri-chile-powder}}), turmeric ({{quantity:ground-turmeric}}), half of the garam masala ({{quantity:garam-masala}}), and black pepper ({{quantity:black-pepper}}).
Cook for 30 seconds, stirring constantly, until the spices are fragrant but not scorched.
Add the crushed tomatoes ({{quantity:crushed-tomatoes}}) and scrape the bottom of the pan to release the browned tomato paste and spices.

stepGroups.title: Simmer the Chickpeas
items: Add the cooked chickpeas, reserved chickpea cooking liquid, and fine sea salt ({{quantity:fine-sea-salt-sauce}}).
Bring to a simmer.
Cook uncovered for 20 to 25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chickpeas absorb the masala and the sauce thickens enough to coat them.
Mash about 1/2 cup of chickpeas against the side of the pan to help thicken the sauce while leaving most chickpeas whole.

stepGroups.title: Finish
items: Turn off the heat.
Stir in the remaining garam masala, lemon juice ({{quantity:lemon-juice}}), and cilantro ({{quantity:fresh-cilantro}}).
Let stand for 5 minutes before serving.
Taste and adjust with salt or lemon juice as needed.
Serve hot with rice, roti, or flatbread.
